mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the effective federal funds rate lowers the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, causing equity investors to bid up the S&P 500 and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% indicates near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or the strategy’s Sharpe ratio falls below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6% signals sufficient slack to suppress wage-driven inflation and extend the timeline for monetary accommodation, which lowers discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the mean forward return in triggered months is not positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production demonstrate broad-based final demand and manufacturing momentum that reduce near-term recession probability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; either proxy alone is insufficient to capture the cross-sector breadth.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not positive in more than 50% of months where both RSAFS and INDPRO increase month-over-month, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During recoveries characterized by high unemployment and rising industrial output, operating leverage surprises dominate equity pricing because sales growth outpaces fixed costs, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; this mechanism weakens once unemployment normalizes.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5.5% and INDPRO rises, positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations, or the strategy underperforms buy-and-hold by more than 200bps annualized over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production from the prior month confirms a positive manufacturing surprise that is quickly impounded into equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as short-term momentum traders chase the output beat.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month increases in INDPRO are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP exceeding its trailing two-year average indicates persistent demand strength that supports upward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 is above its trailing 8-quarter average, 60-day forward returns are negative on average (t-test p < 0.05 for difference from zero) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 1% while consumer prices are rising month-over-month, deeply negative real policy rates penalize cash holdings and drive capital reallocation into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the effect requires the joint presence of low nominal rates and positive inflation momentum.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1.0% and month-over-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is below 1% and CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month, the S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term does not improve prediction versus FEDFUNDS alone (likelihood-ratio test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.6 million units signal robust residential investment and durable goods demand that lifts cyclical earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when HOUST exceeds 1600, or the mean forward return in those months is not statistically greater than zero (p > 0.05).
